data.title: Myseum Pulls Another Tiny-Float AI Pivot Like Allbirds — Stock Soars
data.description:
• MYSE stock is up on heavy volume. See the chart and price action here. 

The rebrand, effective April 15, involves what the company calls a strategic shift toward “privacy-first AI agents” that will be integrated across its social media and messaging apps, including its DatChat encrypted messaging platform and Picture Party photo-sharing app. 

CEO Darin Myman framed the change as a natural extension of the company’s existing tech stack, stating the new name “identifies our core AI-based technology that secures our multi-tiered social media ecosystem.”

The math behind the move tells a familiar story. MYSE has a public float of roughly four million shares, a market cap barely above $6 million, and negative operating cash flow of $4.5 million over the trailing 12 months. 

The stock had been down 16% year-to-date and 40% over the past year before Thursday’s surge, according to Benzinga Pro data. 

In other words, a sub-micro-cap company with minimal revenue and a tiny float added “AI” to its corporate name and immediately doubled in value on heavy volume.

Allbirds’ Playbook

The MYSE comparison to BIRD is almost too clean. 

Allbirds announced Wednesday it would rebrand as “NewBird AI,” pivoting from sneakers to GPU-as-a-Service and AI cloud infrastructure. 

The stock soared as much as 876% intraday and closed up 582% at $16.99. 

But the follow-through tells a cautionary tale: BIRD was trading around $12.82 Thursday morning, down roughly 25% from Wednesday’s close, as the initial euphoria faded.

The initial pop often attracts momentum chasers and short-squeeze volume on a thin float, but once the novelty wears off, traders are left holding shares of a business model that amounts to a press release and a name change.

MYSE was last trading around $1.44 on Wednesday before the after-hours spike. Traders chasing the Thursday move would be wise to watch what happened to BIRD as a real-time preview of how these plays can resolve.

MYSE Price Action: According to Benzinga Pro, Myseum shares were up 110.42%, trading at $3.03 at the time of publication Thursday. 

The stock was bouncing around with a high of $5.76 and a low of $3.41 Thursday, with trading volume of a whopping 153.9 million shares. 

Photo: Vladeep / Shutterstock

data.title: PNC Just Raised Its 2026 Outlook — And The Growth Story May Be Just Getting Started
data.description:
PNC Financial Services Group Inc (NYSE:PNC) Wednesday reported strong results for the first quarter.

The company's strong earnings growth in the quarter was driven both by the FirstBank acquisition and organic growth, according to RBC Capital Markets.

The PNC Financial Services Group Analyst: Analyst Gerard Cassidy maintained an Outperform rating and price target of $235.

The PNC Financial Services Group Thesis: The company reported earnings of $4.32 per share, topping consensus estimate of $4.15 per share, Cassidy said in the note.

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

PNC Financial Services' earnings grew 23%, excluding $98 million of FirstBank integration costs, the analyst stated.

Revenue grew 13% year-on-year to $6.165 billion, driven by strong growth in NII (net interest income) and fee income, "reflecting both the contribution of FirstBank and strong organic commercial loan growth across the legacy franchise," he added.

The analyst mentioned the other highlights of the results as:

  • NII growth of 14% year-on-year and 6.2% sequentially, aided by the full-quarter benefit of FirstBank deposits and loans.
  • Net interest margin expanded 11 basis points sequentially to 2.95%
  • Period-end loans grew 13% year-on-year and 8.6% sequentially to $360.9 billion

For the second quarter, management guided to sequential growth in average loans of 2%-3%, in net interest income of around 3%, and in total revenue of around 3.5%, Cassidy said.

PNC Financial Services raised its 2026 guidance for average loans growth to 11%, from 8% previously and for net interest income to around 14.5%, from 14% previously, while keeping total revenue growth unchanged at around 11%, he added.

"NII guidance of $16.1 billion and NIM guidance of approximately 3.00% exiting 2026 make PNC one of the most compelling NII growth stories in its peer group, in our view," the analyst wrote.

PNC Price Action: PNC Financial Services shares were down 0.11% at $221.82 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Cathie Wood Slams The Brakes — Ark Invest Sits Out As S&P 500 Smashes Records
data.description:
Stock market indexes hit record highs this week with both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 celebrating new highs, despite geopolitical volatility. While many investors were likely actively buying and selling stocks, ETF manager Cathie Wood sat on the sidelines.

Ark Invest Makes No Trades

Ark is among the most transparent companies in the active ETF space, releasing a daily email that lists all the trades made by its main ETFs for the day.

Anyone who receives this email may have noticed something unusual this week.

"There were no trades on 4/15/2026," an email from Ark said on Wednesday night.

The email was the same as the one sent out on Tuesday, which said no trades were made that day. This marks two straight days for Ark Invest not making any stock trades, which is unusual for the company that makes trades daily based on inflows and outflows of funds into the actively managed funds.

Benzinga reached out to Ark Invest to make sure the emails were correct and that no transactions were made.

While the timing of no trades made may be coincidental, the fact that the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 hit new highs while Ark made no trades could prove telling for investors. The company could be waiting for a decline to buy more shares and doesn't feel comfortable selling any current holdings into the rally.

Ark Invest's 2026 Scorecard

The same cannot be said in 2026, with only three of the six ETFs having a positive year-to-date gain and only two outperforming the SPY.

Here are the current year-to-date returns:

In 2025, the Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF was the best-performing of the main Ark Invest ETFs, narrowly beating the Ark Space & Defense Innovation ETF, with gains of 49.8% and 49.2%, respectively.

Those two ETFs are once again leading the way as the only Ark Invest ETFs currently beating the S&P 500 in 2026.

Both of these ETFs are heavily weighted in sectors like AI, robotics and space that saw strong returns in 2025. The two ETFs have seven of their top 10 stocks in common, with the names listed below each in the top 10 of both ETFs.

Given the performance of the two Ark ETFs, those stocks may be names that investors want to monitor for future gains.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Lucid Stock Hits New All-Time Low: What's Pressuring LCID Shares Today?
data.description:
• Lucid Group shares are approaching critical lows. Why did LCID hit a new low?

Uber Robotaxi Deal Raises Execution Pressure

The same update also included a CEO change, with Lucid naming Silvio Napoli as its new CEO while Marc Winterhoff remains COO.

The broader tape is meanwhile leaning positive today, with the Nasdaq-100 up 0.44% and the S&P 500 up 0.21%.

LCID Remains Trapped In A Deep Bearish Trend

Lucid is trading below key trend markers and sitting under its prior swing structure, which keeps the longer-term chart biased to the downside. The stock is trading 20.9% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), suggesting short-term sellers still control the tape, and 30.6% below its 100-day SMA, indicating the intermediate trend remains weak.

The moving average stack is also still bearish, with the 20-day SMA below the 50-day SMA and the 50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA, which is consistent with rallies failing to turn into sustained uptrends. With the 52-week low set at $8.11 and shares currently below that level, price is pressing into "new-low" territory where confidence tends to be fragile.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is bearish with the MACD line below the signal line and a negative histogram, which leans toward fading upside momentum. In everyday terms, MACD staying under its signal line usually means rebounds are struggling to build follow-through.

The stock is down 67.47% over the past 12 months, which matches the picture of a prolonged downtrend rather than a single bad week. Key turning points also show a recent swing high in January and a swing low in April, reinforcing that the stock has been making lower pivots into the spring.

  • Key Resistance: $10.00 — near the 50-day area where rebounds have a higher bar to clear.
  • Key Support: $7.50 — a round-number zone where buyers often try to stabilize a selloff.

Analysts Stay Mixed As Price Targets Shift Lower

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $13.33. Recent analyst moves include:

  • TD Cowen: Hold (Lowers Target to $10.00) (April 15)
  • Baird: Neutral (Lowers Target to $12.00) (April 15)
  • Citigroup: Initiated with Buy (Target $17.00) (March 18)

LCID Shares Slide Thursday

LCID Stock Price Activity: Lucid Group shares were down 7.43% at $7.60 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: courtesy of Lucid

data.title: Anonymous Polymarket Bettors Predict Earnings Better Than Wall Street: Report
data.description:
A report from brokerage Wolfe Research found that when Polymarket users bet a company will miss earnings estimates, the firm misses 44% of the time, more than double the historic 18% benchmark.

When bettors are very confident a company will beat, it happens 90% of the time, above the 81% norm, Bloomberg reported on Thursday.

Yin Luo, who runs quant research at Wolfe, said the accuracy may come from crowdsourcing, with Polymarket users more diverse than the sell-side analyst pool that drives consensus estimates.

A separate working paper from London Business School and Yale researchers offered a more uncomfortable explanation.

The academics found that earnings markets for companies audited by one particular accounting firm were more accurate than those for companies with other auditors. They declined to name the firm, but the finding raises questions about whether some participants may be trading on material non-public information.

Insider Trading Scrutiny Is Nothing New

Prediction markets have been dogged by insider trading allegations for months.

In February, a former MrBeast editor was banned from Kalshi after racking up a near-perfect trading record on contracts tied to the YouTube star’s videos.

Federal prosecutors opened a probe into suspiciously timed bets on Polymarket around the Maduro capture and the opening strikes of the Iran war.

The CFTC under Chairman Michael Selig has also warned retail traders about the risks of insider activity on federally regulated contracts.

Prediction Market Volumes Are Booming

Earnings markets remain a sliver of prediction market activity, with just $795,315 in volume on Polymarket last week, or 0.03% of the total.

The LBS and Yale academics found earnings-market bettors tend to make money on prediction markets while most traders lose, suggesting the group is a relatively skilled subset.

Total industry volumes are booming. Kalshi and Polymarket combined for $6.02 billion during the week of April 6 to 11, a new all-time high, with industry volume crossing $6.5 billion. The Masters Tournament alone drove $545 million in trading on Kalshi, the biggest single sports event in the company’s history.

And the growth may only be starting. Bernstein analysts recently projected prediction market volumes will reach $1 trillion by 2030.

If anonymous traders are already outpacing Wall Street’s sell-side on earnings calls with volumes under $800,000 a week, the signal may sharpen considerably once earnings contracts move from sliver to mainstream.

Image: Shutterstock
